output:
**Yes**, arriving to school on time is more likely overall.

Using the law of total probability:

P(on time) = P(on time | wake on time) × P(wake on time) + P(on time | wake late) × P(wake late)

P(on time) = 0.52 × 0.95 + 0.87 × 0.05 = 0.494 + 0.0435 = **0.5375**

The overall probability of arriving on time is **53.75%**, which exceeds 50%, so arriving on time is more likely than arriving late.
